calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joel Blackman for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Blackman gave the history of the case, stated that the abuser purchased the properties and stocks, that there are IRS and Franchise Tax Board liens, read from the Public Administrator's notes from the year 2000 stating that the property was looked at, that it is in Lake County in which property is not doing well, that Mr. Clark had taken out an ad at the time to sell the property even if it was only $1.00, that there were no bidders, that they spoke with an agent regarding selling the property now, that in communicating with agents in the area found that no agent will take it, that the properties are not buildable, that they are filled with trash, that the liens are more than the property value and that the County will not take it back. The Court stated that it will take the Petition for Instructions under submission and requested that Mr. Blackman file the documents he referenced. Mr. Blackman stated that he only has a copy not originals and that it was signed by Mr. Clark who is long gone. The Court requested that it be provided.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
